This company was registered in Edinburgh July 14th, 1876, as the Clyde Coil Co., Ltd., to carry on coal mining operations at Hamilton, in Scotland. In 1892 the company acquired the business and collieries of John Wilson, when the name of the company was changed. In 1894 the company acquired the lease of a coalfield on the estate of Glencraig, near Lochgelly, Fifeshire. The collieries are known as Shawfield, Brownlee, Law, GiUhead, DouglM Park, E. Parkhead, Clyde, Wellhall, and Glencraig Fife , covering 3,350 acres; also Croashill Estates, adjoining Glencraig, acquired in 1902. The company ha also acquired the lease of the Saline and Outh coalfields, Fifeshire, about 8,1W acres, near the new Bosyth Naval Base ; a railway three miles long has been constructed to develop this property. Plant is being erected and on the Onti portion boring operations are in progress. The authorised capital is £230,000, in 4,000 seven per cent, cumulative fir preference and 1,000 seven per cent, cumulative second preference shares of £M each and 60,000 ordinary shares of £3 each ; all the shares are issued, 1,791 first preference and 60,000 ordinary being fully paid, and 2,209 first preference and all the second preference having £5 called, making the paid-up capital £213.955. The capital, originally £150,000, subsequently inoreased to £190,000, in preference and ordinary shares, was reduced in 1884 to £85,000 by writing off £7 per ordinary share, and the preference dividend was reduced from 10 to 7 per cent. In 1898 the capital was increased to £170,000 by creation of the second preference and 25,000 new ordinary shares. The second preference were allotted to shareholders at par, and the new ordinary at £2 premium. In 1894 the capital was increased to present amount by creation and issue of 20,000 ne w ordinary shares to share- . . . miscellaneous companies. 1061 WILSONS AND CLYDE COAL oontlnued. holders. The consideration was £250,000, paid £120,160 in cash, £29,840 in fully-paid preference, and £100,000 in fully-paid ordinary shares. The oompany was promoted by the vendors, the Windsor Steam Coal Co., Ltd., who guaranteed the preference dividend for two years. Sinking is completed, and the colliery is working. The authorised capital is £275,000, in 12,500 six per cent, cumulative preference with priority for capital , and 15,000 ordinary shares of £10 each ; all the preference and 10,000 ordinary shares are issued and fully paid, the ordinary and 2,984 preference being issued credited as fully paid. There are £125,000 five per cent, registered first mortgage debentures in bonds of £100, secured by trust deed, constituting a floating charge on the undertaking and redeemable after January 1st, 1909, by annual or other drawings at 106 per cent. ; interest is payable on January 1st and July 1st; the first surplus profits, after payment of preferential dividend, are to be applied as to 3 per cent, on the amount of first debentures towards forming a fund for their redemption, and the dividend on the ordinary shares is limited to 10 per cent., until a fund amounting to £60,000 has been formed and invested in the name of the trustee to debenture-holders. There are also £20,000 five per cent, second debentures in bonds of £100 each, issued in October, 1904. Secretary and Offices William Geznmell, 75, Bothwell Street, Glasgow. Telegrams : Elcoal, Glasgow. This company was registered in Edinburgh July 14th, 1876, as the Clyde Coal Co., Ltd., to carry on coal mining operations at Hamilton, in Scotland. Tn 1892 the company acquired the Business and collieries ef John Wilson, when the name of the company was changed. In 1894 the compauy acquired the lease of a coalfield on the estate of Glencraig, near Lochgelly, Fifeshire. The collieries are known as Shawfield, Brownlee, Law, Gillhead, Douglas Park, E. Farkhead, Clyde, and Glencraig Fife , covering 3,350 acres; also Crosshill Estates, adjoining Glencraig, acquired in 1902. The company has also acquired the lease of the Saline and Outh coalfields, Fifeshire, about 3,100 acres, near the new Kosyth Naval Base ; a railway three miles long has been constructed to develop this property. Plant is being erected, and the Outh portion has been tested by boreholes. The authorised capital is £230,000, in 4,000 seven per cent, cumulative first preference and 1,000 seven per cent, cumulative second preference shares of £10 each and 60,000 ordinary shares of £3 each j all the shares are issued, 1,791 first preference and 60,000 ordinary being fully paid, and 2,209 first preference and all the second preference having £5 called, making the paid-up capital £213,955. The capital, originally £150,000, subsequently increased to £190,000, in preference and ordinary shares, was reduced in 1884 to £85,000 by writing off £7 per ordinary share, and the preference dividend was reduced from 10 to 7 per cent. In 1892 the capital was increased to £170,000 by creation of the second preference and 25,000 new ordinary shares. The second preference were allotted to shareholders at par, and the new ordinary at £2 premium. In 1894 the capital was increased to present amount by creation and issue of 20,000 new ordinary shares to share holders. The uncalled preference capital is payable when required atone month s notice. Dividends paid on ordinary shares : per ct. text: report was dated May 17th, 1907. SNYMAN S DRIFT EXPLORATION. LIMITED Manager R. H. Murray. | Offices Box 2667, Johannesburg. This company was registered in the Transvaal in March, 1906, to acquire the freehold farm 8nyman s Drift No. 4, area 3,028 morgon, situate in the Zontpansberg distriot of the Transvaal. The consideration was £17,500 in fullypaid shares. The African and European Investment Co., Ltd., was interested in the sale. A 10-stamp mill has been erected and crushing commenced Aug., 1907. The authorised capital is £25,000, in 25,000 shares of £1 each ; 22,500 shares are issued and fully paid including vendors . SOCIETA EREYTRHITRREAEAGOLPDERMINLINEG CMOIMNPIEANRYE . D ORO Direotors. General Baron Michele de Renzis, Chairman. | S. Symons, Vice- Chairman. Prince D. A. Doria-Pamphilj. | E. Talamo, C.E. I B. A. Nathan, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.1LM, Secretary and Head Offices Carlo Casati, Rome, Italy. London Secretary and Offices J. M. Spankie, 7, Adams Court. E.C. Telegrams : Eritrea, London. N.T., 9776 Wall. P.O.T., 8767 Central. This company was registered in the Italian colony of Erythrea on June 24th, 1900, to take over a mining concession granted by the Italian Government over lands situate in Erythrea, North Abyssinia. The property consists of three blocks, aggregate area of 117 square miles, on the Asmara Plateau, including the township of Asmara capital of the colony , distant about 70 miles from the port of Massowah. Development is proceeding, and supplies of ore are being opened up. The authorised capital is 2,000,000 lire £80,000 , in 80,000 shares to bearer of 25 lire £1 each ; all the shares are issued and fully paid. In 1906 the shares were written down to 12 5 lire each, and 40,000 new shares of 25 lire each were offered to shareholders at the rate of one new for every two old shares held ; the uld shares were then converted into shares of 25 lire each, shareholders receiving one for every two held. Accounts for year endod December 31st, 1905, submitted in Borne May, 1906, showed cash 3,514 liro ; on current account 192,407 francs, Government bonds 98,950 lire j debtors 10,547 lire ; croditors 19,014 liro ; reserve fund 84,153 lire. No later accounts received. somaTjlanIO S This company was registered June 8th, 1905 see vol. for 1906 . On September 23rd, 1907, it was resolved to wind up voluntarily; liquidator, H. A. H. Russell, Capel House, New Broad Street, E.C. SOOWIN CONCESSIONS, LIMITED. Directors Qualification, £100 . Arthur W. Cohen, Chairman. | Joseph Woolf Cohen. Solicitors Morris, Coote & Co. Bankers Commercial Bauk of Scotland, Limited. | Auditor F. Clifford Goodman. Secretary and Offlces- G. F. Gibbons, 11, Queen Victoria Street, E.C. N.T., 8187 Bank. 33 514 . SOOWIN CONCESSIONS This company was registered on August 28th, 1901, to acquire, by agreement with the Ashanti Three Rivers Synd., Ltd., the Soowin concession, about 144 square miles, between Broomassie and Tarkwa.Wassau district, Gold Coast Colony. The consideration was £75,000, paid £10,000 in cash and £65,000 in fully -paid shares. Other properties known as Nyinaa Bippo and Bogydayau have been purchased for £15,000 in fully-paid shares. Amalgamation with other companies is under consideration. The authorised capital is £100,000, in 100,000 shares of £1 each ; 90,000 shares are issued and fully paid, including 65,000 vendors . No accounts yet issued. Transfer form, common ; fee, 2s. 6d. The 22nd May, 1902, was appointed a settling day in shares Nos. 50,001 to 75,000. SOUD AN DEVELO PM ENT AND EXPLORATION COMPANY, LIMITED. Managers New Egyptian Co., Ltd., London, Paris and Cairo. Consulting Engineer J. 8. Beresford. I Manager at Cairo H. B. Greville. Manager and Offices at Khartoum, Soudan Harold P. Hall, Khedive Avenue. Solicitors Hollams, Sons, Coward & Hawksley. Bankers Union of London & Smiths Bank. Limited. | Auditors Cooper Brothers & Co. Local Secretary and Offices Th. Nossif, Rue Daram alii. Box 79 , Cairo. Paris Office 7, Rue Meyerbeer. Secretary and Offices Thomas Day, 31, Clement s Lane, E.C. N.T., 986 Avenue. This company was registered on March 20th, 1900, and acquired a concession from the Soudan Government for a service of steamers and barges, under guarantee from the Government, to trade on the Blue and White Niles. These boats now run a regular service, and a trading business is also being established. The business of the company has been practically taken over by the New Egyptian Co., Ltd., by an exohange of shares. The authorised capital is £100,000, in 97,500 ordinary shares of £1 each and 100,000 deferred shares of 6d. each ; 67,500 ordinary and all the deferred shares are issued and fully paid. The deferred shares are entitled to one-half the surpluB profits in each year, after a dividend of 6 per cent, has been paid on the ordinary shares for Buch year. There are £50,000 five per cent, navigation bonds debentures authorised, of which £32,500 are issued. The accounts for year to October 31st, 1906, submitted on October 28th, 1907, showed £14,861 to debit of income and expenditure account, an inorease of £3,753 on the year. Transfer form, common ; fee, 2s. 6d. SOUTH AFRICAN COLLIE Rl ES. LI MTTED.

THIS company was registered on August 1st, 1899, to acquire as a going concern two groups of copper mines locally known as Pilou and A0, situate in the Diahot district of the French Colony of New Caledonia, an island in the Pacific Ocean, to the north-east of Sydney, New South Wales. The mines cover an estimated area of over 4,000 acres. The Pilou has been extensively developed and con nected by tunnelling with the A0, which is situate at a distance of about 2 miles. The ores consist chiefly of sulphide of copper. The purchase considera tion was £700,000, and was paid in cash, the vendors to this company being the International Copper Corporation, Limited, who had been developing the property for nearly three years. smelting operations were commenced in 1901. The properties are held by another company, but this company holds all its shares see Les Mines de Cuivre, Pilou, Limited . The authorised capital is £760,000, in 150,000 shares of £5 each ,- all the shares have been issued and are fully paid. In August, 1899, all the shares were ofl ered for public subscription, at par, by the London and Globe Finance Corporation, Limited, and allotted, priority of allotment being given to share holders of that Corporation. In May, 1901, an ofler was made to shareholders of £75,000 part of £100,000 authorised 7 per cent. first mortgage debentures, at 95 per cent., in multiples of £1, secured by a trust deed upon the general assets of the company, and redeemable within five years at 105 per cent. either by yearly drawings out of profits, or upon three months notice; for every £5 subscribed, a bonus of one fully-paid share was given, certain shareholders having surrendered shares for this purpose; £46,000 debentures are allotted. Telegrams : Dolorosoa, London. Phone : 607 £ 627 Bank. This company was incorporated under the laws of the State of Maine, U.S.A., in 1904 and owns a gold and silver mining property covering 227£ acres in the Guerrero district, Chihuahua, Mexico, also 10,000 acres surface rights ; a milling, slimes and Butters filter plant are erected and at work and bullion shipments are in progress. Ore reserves November 1st, 1907, 52,110 tons, value $2,425,136. The authorised capital is $1,980,000, in 396,000 shares of $5 each ; all the shares are issued and fully paid. Dividends paid : 1907, 3 per cent. February 25th j 3 per cent. May 25th ; 3 per cent. August 24th ; 3 per cent. November 25th ; 1908, 3 per cent. February 25th ; 3 per cent. May 25th ; 3 per cent. August 25th ; 3 per cent. November 25th. The accounts to November 30th, 1907, showed $370,028 credit carried forward after paying $267,276 dividends February 1907 to November 1907 . Transfers accepted at London Office for transmission to head office at a fee of 4s. 2d. per 100 shares, and 3s. per cent, insurance, splitting certificate of less than 100 shares, 5d. per certificate extra. The 17th September, 1908, was appointed settling day in London in 396, COO Bhares. Latest price, 1 ,v . DOMAINS COMPANY, LIMITED. This company was registered December 19th, 1903 see vol. for 1908 . Ross a freehold mineral property, situate near Santo Domingo, Island of Santo Domingo. The consideration was £149,993 in fully-paid shares. The authorised capital is £150,000, in 150,000 shares of £1 each originally of 5s. each ; all the shares are issued and fully paid. No accounts received. DOMINION COPPER COMPANY, LIMITED. Offices PhoBuix, British Columbia. THIS company was registered under the laws of British Columbia in 1905. and owns copper, gold and silver mining properties, area 3 10 acres, at RoSBland, British Columbia. The principal mines are the Brooklyn, Idaho, Rawhide, Crown Silver, Athelstan and Sunset. Smelting plant is erected and is capable of treating from 050 to 1,500 tons per day. The authorised capital is $5,000,000, in 500,000 shares of $10 each ; 320,003 shares are issued and fully paid. There are $700,000 six per cent, bonds, part of $1,000,000. This company was registered December 13th, 1906, and is a reconstruction of a company of similar name registered October 3rd, 1894 see vol. for 1906 . The properties comprise 175,743 acres freehold land, with mineral rights, subject to royalty, and 1,220,842 acres pastoral leasehold land, situate near Ooolgardie, Western Australia. A block of 18,800 acres was sold by the former company to the Block 45 Hampton Plains Estate, Ltd., and another portion, known as Block 42, G6 . HAMPTON PLAINS ESTATE 1906 - sontiiiued. to the Hampton Gold Fields, Ltd. Several other blocks of freehold land have been sold to various companies ; the extent of freehold land now held is as above. The company also holds a share interest in Victorian Deep Lead gold mining properties and in the Bed Hill company. In 1895 certain rights were sold to the Coolgardie Waterworks, Ltd. see vol. for 1900 , but in January, 1901, it was decided to purchase the undertaking of that company and the waterworks is now owned by this company, but the revenue has almost ceased owing to Government supply; the company has also brickworks, township, Ac. Mining operations were formerly chiefly confined to the Merry Hampton and Queen Mines, but results were disappointing. The Lady Millar Mine at Norseman is being developed, and crushing commenced September, 1907. The company has acquired from the Hampton Properties, Ltd., the lease for 7 years of Block 50, with an option to purchase the whole or part. On reconstruction the oompany held 27,009 fullypaid shares in Bed Hill, Westralia, Gold Minos, Ltd., 69,447 in Block 42, 90,313 in Hampton Properties, Ltd., and sundry other shares. The authorised capital is £200,000, in 400,000 shares of 10s. each ; 232,083 shares £116,042 10s. are issued and 9s. paid. There is £99,560 de benture stock issued in exchange for debenture stock in old company, with rights as below : Under the soheme of reconstruction shareholders were offered one share oredited as 7s. 6d. paid for each fully-paid 16s. share in old oompany, the liability being payable 6d. on application, 6d. on March 31st, 1907, and the balance in calls of 6d. at intervals of not less than six months. The debenture stock bears interest at four per cent, per annum, payable until November 30th, 1910 out of the revenue to be obtained from assets taken over from the old company, but in any year when the profits are suffloient to pay ten per cent, on the share capital the interest is to be six per cent. The stock is redeemable at par by annual drawings of £5,000 commencing January, 1912, and the proceeds of the sale of any of the assets are to be applied towards redemption ; holders have the right to convert into fully -paid shares at par at any time. This company was registered in Melbourne, Victoria, on August 11th, 1903, to take over the undertakings of a former company of same name and the North Mount Lyell Copper Co., Ltd. The former Mount Lyell Mining and Railway Co., Ltd., was registered in Melbourne, Victoria, in 1898, being a reconstruction of a former company of same name formed to ac quire copper mining leases comprising 333 aoree situate at Mount Lyell, about 18 miles from. Macquarie Harbour, on the west coast of Tasmania, also 43 acres for reduction works site. The ore of the mines consists of a mixture of iron and copper pyrites with silver and gold. A special lease for 30 years was granted by the Tasmania Legislature in September, 1893. The company constructed a railway from the mines to Macquarie Harbour. In August, 1900, the adjoining property of the South Tharsis Mining Co., No Liability, was purchased for £45,000 cash; the adjoining Royal Tharsis Mine, area 40 acres, was also acquired at a total cost of £13,347, and the King Lyell Mine at a cost of £2,000. In March, 1902, M.L. Reserve leases and Gleo Lyell leases, and in September, 1902, North Crown Lyell Block No. 602-93 06 acres and Central Lyell Block 47G 10 acres were also acquired. The North Mount Lyell Copper Co., Ltd., was registered on 20th September, 1897, to acquire the property of the North Mount Lyell Mining Co., No Liability, of Melbourne vol. for 1002 , consisting of 80 acres of mining land, in three contiguous blocks of 10 acres each, situate in the northern portion of the Mount Lyell field, Tasmania, and held under mining leases from the Crown; also the right to construct a railway 28 miles long from the mines to Macquarie Harbour. The chief product is copper, but the mine also yields gold and silver. The purchase consideration was £305,000, in 395,000 fully-paid shares. The railway wbb constructed, and wharf, pier and jetty at Macquarie Harbour completed. The company also erected smelting works. The adjoining property of the Lyell Tharsis Mining Co. see vol. for 1905 has been purchased for £2,000, and the company now owns 4,108 acres in all. Smelting was commenced by former company in June, 1896, and 11 furnaces are now at work, also plant for treating converter matte. The company has also erected works at Yarraville for the manufacture of superphosphates, and a site has also been secured at Port Adelaide, 8. Australia. For half-year to September 30th, 1906, 199,289 tons treated produced 4,491 tons blister copper, containing 4,444 tons copper, 829,188ozs. silver and 10,746ozs. gold ; costs 15s. 10 4d. per ton. For half-year to March 3 1st, 1907, 206,741 tons produced 3,839 tons blister copper, containing 3,796 tons copper, 355,372ozs. silver and 10,115ozs. gold ; costs 14s. 4-9d. per ton. For half-year to September 30th, 1907, 213,275 tons produced 4,148 tons blister copper, containing 4,090 tons copper, 344,715 ozs. silver and 9,334oxs. gold i costs 15s. 5 2d. per ton. Lyell, 438,020 tons value 5 75 per cent, oopper, T33 ozs. silver and 005 ozs. gold per ton. Forcrushings see table at commencement of volume. The authorised capital is £1,300,000, in 1,300,000 shares of £1 each ; 1,200,000 shares have been issued credited as fully paid in exchange for shares of the former companies. There are £40,200 balance of £140,000 5 per oent. first mortgage debentures, issued in exchange for the North Mount Lyell debentures. The following were the terms of the amalgamation scheme : One fully-paid share for each North share absorbing 600,000 , 2fT shares for each Mount Lyell share of £3 absorbing 600,000 ; North debentures exchanged for new debentures of similar amount ; 4s. per share £68,750 cash bonus to Mount Lyell shareholders before amalgamation ; final cash payments of 3d. per share to Mount Lyell share holders and 2d. per share to North shareholders by liquidators. This company was registered June 25th, 1897, to take over the undertakings of the African Metals Co , Ltd., registered July 12th, 1895 see vol. for 1899 , and the Mines Selection Co., Ltd., registered May 21st, 1895 see vol. for 1899 . The company took over some Witwatersrand deep level claims, farms, Ac, in the Transvaal, and shares in South African, Australasian, Amerioan, and British Columbian companies, including large interest in Brakpan Mines, Ltd., acquired in 1904. The company floated privately the Anglo-Klondyke Mining Co., Ltd. In October, 1905, tho company acquired the Johannesburg interests of Messrs. A. Dunkelsbuhler & Co., for 400,000 fully-paid shares. The authorised capital is £1,200,000, in shares of £1 each ; 1,000,000 shares are issued and fully paid, 210,942 in exchange for Bhares of the old companies. In October, 1905, the capital was increased to present amount, 400,000 shares being issued to Messrs. Dunkelsbuhler & Co., and 200,000 held in reserve for conversion of debentures. In November, 1905, £400,000 five per cent, first mortgage debentures to bearer of £20 and £100 each were offered to shareholders at par, the issue being guaranteed by the Bank fur Handol und Industrie at a commission of 2, per cent.; holders may, at any time up to December 31st, 1908, convert into fully-paid shares at £2 per share; debentures may be redeemed after December 31st, 1908, and before December 31st, 1913, at 5 per cent, premium, and are redeemable after 1913 by 15 annual drawings ; the company may at any time purchase deben tures in the market at 105 or less per cent. This company was registered January 12th, 1895, to acquire the undertaking of the Bhodesia Development and Exploration Synd., Ltd. The company has been concerned in the notation of the Bulawayo Market, Chioago-Qaika, Bhodesia Matabeleland, Gaika Gold, DavieB Selukwe, Lomagunda Development, Loinagunda Beefs, Mayfair Development, Scottish Mashonaland, Ayrshire Gold, Revue Mauicaland, Grand Champion, Edmundian and other companies. In 1897 the assets of the South African Investors Synd., Ltd., were purchased for 6,000 fully-paid shares. The agency business carried on by the Gold Fields of Matabeleland, Ltd., in Bhodesia, has also been acquired. In 1903, the assets of the Filabusi Charterland Goldfields, Ltd., were pnrchased for 1,000 shares. In December, 1906, it was deoided to extend the company s operations outside Africa. Properties held at 30th June, 1906 : Claims owned 450, partly owned 435 ; land owned 66,443 moreen, also interests in a further 92,304 mcrgen, equal in all to about 314,750 acres ; 3 building stands in Salisbury, 5 in Bulawayo and 1 in Tati ; various shares, including large holdings in the Consolidated African Copper Trust, Ayrshire Gold, Rbodesian Banket and West Rhodeaian Banket companies. The authorised capital is £257,000, in 257,000 shares of £1 each ; 254,231 shares are issued and fully paid. The capital was formerly £100,000 ; 16,411 resorve shares were offered to shareholders in 1898, at £5 5s. each and 1,532 were sold at £5 Is. 9d. The capital was increased to £150,000 in 1899, and to 175,000 in 1901 ; 25,000 shares were issued to shareholders at £4 10s. each in 1900, and 27,135 at £4 10s. in 1901. In 1902, the capital was increased to £225,000, the 50,000 new Bhares being offered to shareholders at £5 each ; and in 1904 inoreased to £252,000, and subsequently to present amount. Dividends paid : for year 1899-0, 10 per cent, in cash and 40 per cent, in fullypaid shares of the Bulawayo Market and Offices Co., Ltd., distributed February 1900 ; for year 1901-2, 10 per cent, in cash and 25 per cent, in fully -paid shares of Gatling Hill Co., in February, 1902, and five per cent, in December, 1902, making 40 per cent, for year j interim on account of 1902-3, 20 per cent, in cash and 25 per oent. in fully -paid shares of the Bulawayo Market and Offices Co., declared December 4th, 1902. The accounts for year ended June 30th, 1905, submitted December 21st, 1905, showed credit balance increased from £101,071 to £104,242, after adding £83,796 share premiums received during the year, and writing £155,632 off shareholdings. This company was registered June 22nd, 1894, and is a reconstruction of the Van Ryn Estate and Gold Mining Co., Ltd. see vol. for 1896 . The property now owned is situated on the farm Vlakfontein No. 442, in the Eastern district of the Witwatersrand Gold Fields, and is 7,944 acres in extent, in addition to an unproclaimed area of 534 morgen recently acquired. The mining area on the Van Eyn reef, the ore body which is being worked, consists of some 136 intact reef claims after allowing for the ground exhausted, while the company s waterrights cover 351$ acres. Crushing commenced in February, 1892, and in October, 1896, a new 80-stamp mill and cyanide plant were started. Another 80-stamp mill was acquired with the Van Ryn West property, making 160 stamps. A new slimes plant has been added and four tube mills are being erected. Crushing was suspended in October, 1899, and during the war the machinery was damaged by the Boers, but this has been replaced, and crushing was resumed in June, 1903. During 1906-7, 266,940 tons yielded gold value £496,686 or 37s. 16d. per ton at a cost of 21s. 9-2d. and a profit of 15s. 4-4d. per ton ; during 1907-8 to December, 1907 , 135,642 tons crushed yielded gold value £238,472 and a profit of £101,659. The vendors received £45,000 in cash and £150,000 in shares. In January, 1889, the company sold 36 claims for £210,000 in cash and £15,000 in shares of the Balmoral Main Reef Gold Mining Co., Ltd. In May, 1893, 12 claims adjoining the Glencairn property, with the corresponding 48 dip claims, making 60 in all, were sold to Barnato Bros, for £60,000. The company now owns Mynpacht No. 320, 296 morgen in extent, 78 prospecting and two diggers claims and 4,780 morgen freehold on farm Drietfontein, No. 148, Boksburg district, Transvaal, and also derives a revenue of about £11,000 per annum from a residential estate of 4,892 acres, situate on the said farm to the north of the main reef series. Crushing commenced in January, 1888, with a 50-stamp mill, and further 50 stamps were afterwards added, but crushing was suspended in 1891 and the mine has since been thoroughly developed and a new 120-stamp mill and cyanide plant erected, crushing being resumed in 1896. One lease is for 30 years from August 4th, 1902, of mining blocks No. 1, Nabapoor, 160 acres, No. 2, Kabulayatkatti orKabligatti , 160 acres, and No. 3, Sortur, 160 acres, subject to half-yearly rental of 240 rupees, surface rent of 8 annas per acre, and royalty of 7 per cent, of net profits from gold extracted above the amount of half-yearly rental ; the other lease is for 30 years from January pt, 1904, of mining Block No. 4, Attikatti and Sortur, 160 aores, subject to half-yearly rental of 80 rnpees, and surface rent and royalty as above. This company was registered in Edinburgh July 14th, 1876, as the Clyde Coil Co., Ltd., to carry on coal mining operations at Hamilton, in Scotland. In 1892 the company acquired the business and collieries of John Wilson, when the name of the company was changed. In 1894 the company acquired the lease of a coalfield on the estate of Glencraig, near Lochgelly, Fifeshire. The collieries are known as Shawfield, Brownlee, Law, GiUhead, DouglM Park, E. Parkhead, Clyde, Wellhall, and Glencraig Fife , covering 3,350 acres; also Croashill Estates, adjoining Glencraig, acquired in 1902. This company was reaistered March 6th, 1906, as the Burma Mines, Railway and Smelting Co., Ltd., to acquire the undertaking of the Great Eastern Mining Co., Ltd. see vol. far 1908 , owning a group of ancient silver lead mines including smelting and other plant and partly constructed railway at Baudwen, in the Northern Shan States, Upper Burma, 60 miles from the frontier of the Province of Ynnnan, China, and about 45 miles from Hsipaw station, on the Lashio Branch of the Burma railways. The property is held under licence from the Indian Government, and covers an area of 2,496 acres. The consideration was £309,903 £259,993 for property, and £50,000 for smelter railway, Ac , payable £50,000 in debenture stock and £259,993 in fully-paid ordinary shares. The properties were reported on by Bewick, Moreing & Co., Salman and Picard, and F. D. Chase. The name was changed in April, 1908. The railway, having a length of about 50 miles, conveys the ore from the mines at Baudwen to Manpwe Junction on the Burma Railways, and from thence is transported to the company s smelter at Mandnlay. The authorised capital is £450,000, in 450,000 ordinary shares of 2s. each and 405,000 deferred shares Bf £1 each ; 260,000 ordinary and 260,000 deferred 650 THE Uimm UANVAL. BDBMA MINES, LIMITED shares are issued and fully paid. The capital was increased from £410,000 present amount in November, 1907, and was altered to present form in April, 1908, 45,000 ordinary £1 shares being converted into 450,000 shares of 2s. the remaining shares being called deferred shares ; 260,000 ordinary were offered to deferred shareholders for subscription, the remaining 190,000 being reserved for debenture-holders rights. The ordinary shares are entitled to 95 per cent, profits and assets. Tnrs company was first registered in the Transvaal September 8th, 1887, as the Bantjes Reef Gold Mining Co., L td., to acquire 104 gold-miningelaims on the Farms Vogelstruisfontein and Langlaagtc, WitwaterBrand, Transvaal The vendors received £70,000 in fully-paid shares. In December, 1888, 16 claims on the Langlaagte Farm, with adjoining inynpacht 33 morgen, were sold for £60,000 cash. In 1893 six south claims were purchased for £1,150. On September 9th, 1895, the company was reconstructed in the Transvaal under the present title, various adjoin ing properties being acquired for 196,250 shares . A further 25 claims were after wards acquired for 16,700 shares. Early in 1896, the company purchased for £76,000 cash the freehold of 3,148 acres, comprising about half of the farm Vogel struisfontein known as Van der Hoven s portion, including the part on which the company s mining claims arc situated, also including the Old and New Florida Township and the Florida Lake water-right. Mining area now held, 813 claims, also four water-rights. Three shafts are being sunk, and at September 30th, 1909, the east incline shaft has been sunk to a depth of 1,650 feet, the central shaft to 810 feet and the west shaft to C48 feet ; ore reserves at September 80th, 1909, 580,707 tons, value 6 4dwts. per ton. A 70-stamp battery tube mill, and cyanide and slimes plant are being erected, and crushing is expected to commence about Maroh, 1910. Provided the development operations continue satisfactory the estimated life will exceed 40 years treating 120,000 tons yearly. A revenue is derived from claim licences on the freehold and from stands in the Old Florida Township j the New portion is about to be dealt with. The authorised capital is £322,123, in 322,123 shares of £1 each; 306031 shares are issued and fully paid. Under the chome of reconstruction in 1895, five fully-paid new shares were given for every four old shares, with the right to subscribe for one new share at 50s. The guarantors took 36,250 shares at 50s., in return for an option over 60,000 reserve shares at 60s. This option was exorcised in September, 1896. In August, 1908, the capital, formerly £435,000, was reduced to £108,588 by cancelling 650 reserve shares and issuing 1 share for every 4 shares held : it was then increased to £295.200 and 140,000 shares offered pro rata to shareholders at par, being 8 for every £5 old hares held ; the issue was guaranteed ; subscribers received an option to November 11th, 1910, at 22 . 6d. per share over the same number of shares subscribed for, such rights being covered by a separate nominative transferable certificate ; the guarantors have the right to take up any shares at 22b. 6d. not taken up by shareholders under the option. The capital was increased to present amount in November, 1909. In May, 1909, 30,000 shares were taken up by H. Eckstein & Co., at 75s. each. The former company .was a reconstruction of the Minerals Assets Co., Ltd., registered November 8th, 1898 see vol. for 1905 , to acquire the property of the Appalachian Tin Mine, Ltd. since sold , and afterwards acquired the assets of the New Russell 0 ld and Exploration, Ltd., comprising two gold-mining claims known as the California and Oregon, situate about one mile west of Cripple Creek, Colorado, U.8.A On reconstruction in 1903 a new property was acquired, consisting of 50 gold -mining claims known as the Mexican, situate about 2 miles east of Eureka Station in the Barberton district of the Transvaal. text: of . a R. M. Wilson, Chairman. I 564 4: . ROBERT ADDIE £5 10s. each and 15,000 shares of £9 each; all the shares are issued and fully paid The original capital was £250,000 in 10,000 preference and 15,000 ordinary shares of £10 each, the preference being offered for public subscrition in January, 1893; it was reduced in 1899 to £235,000 by writing off £1 per ordinary share loss on collieries abandoned, and in December, 1900, the preference capital was reduced from £100,000 to £70,000 by paying off £3 per share, and on May 31, 1913, a further sum of £1 105. was repaid on each preference share, making capital as above. Dividends In addition to a cumulative preferential dividend of 6 per cent. per annum the preference shares were entitled. so long as the reference share capital stood at £100,000, to one-third of the divisible profit remaining over a ter payment of a non-cumulative dividend of 6 per cent. on the ordinary shares, but in February, 1907, it was resolved that the residue be divisible between both classes pro min. To provide for the gradual reduction of the preference share capital, there is to be set aside annually as a special reserve out of the profits remaining over after payment of the preference dividend, and before any dividend is paid on the ordina shares, a sum of £5,000, to be applied in the rateable reduction of the preference ca ital, suzb annual payment being cumulative. The company may not create or issue any de ntures or debenture stock, or any preference shares to rank either preferably to or pan panu with the preference shares nowissued, without the consent of the holders of three-fourths of the preference capital. Registered January 9, 1906, in the Transvaal, and has acquired a 25 years, lease, with right of renewal for a further 25 years, over 500 acres, on Farm Damplaats, No. 319, 20 miles north-east of Boshof. Registered September 19, 1894, in the Transvaal, as the Robinson Deep, Ltd., to acquire from the Goldfields Deep, Ltd., about 148 main reef dip claims south of the Robinson Mine, Witwatersrand, Transvaal. The purchase consideration was £100,000, in fully-paid shares. A further 86 claims on the dip of the Worcester and Ferreira mines were afterwards purchased from the Paarl Ophir Co., for £10,000 cash, and £25,000 in fully-paid shares. The company was reconstructed and registered in the Transvaal under the present title on July 15, 1899. In January, 1910, 1 03 claims were acquired for £36,703, and early in 1911, undermining rights covered by an area equal to 5 27 claims were acquired from the Government. The considera tion was £28,998 per annum for a period of ten years, commencing September, 1911. As from October 1, 1913, £2,181 per month out of profits is to be placed to a reserve gold account for the purpose of amortisation of the said claims. The company now owns 215 claims, 193 situate on farm Turffontcin No. 21 and 17 on Booysen s Estate No. 20, and the freehold surface rights of 13 morgen, 287 square roods, on Turffontein No. 21, known as Clear Springs, Johannesburg, and 527 claims held on lease from the Government as mentioned above. Crushing commenced in April. 1898, with 40 stamps; 260 have since been added, making 300 stamps in all; ten tube mills and cyanide and slimes plant are also erected. It is proposed to gradually discard half the stamps and increase the tube mills to 13, parts of the discarded stamps to be used for renewals. The capacity will thus be raised from 50,000 to 65,000 tons per month. During 1913-14, 619,140 tons yielded £851,623; profit £324,725, or 10s. 6d. per ton. Registered 1887, in the Transvaal, to acquire a lease of a portion of the farm Turffontcin, Witwatersrand, with a mynpacht of about 220 acres on the Main Reef. In 1891, the company acquired the freehold of the myn pacht for £15,000 In 1893, 30 southern claims were acquired for £120,000 cash. In 1898, 6 16 claims were sold to the Robinson Central Deep, Ltd. now Crown Mines , for 45,089 fully-paid shares and the right to subscribe for 15,030 shares at £2.
